---
title: |
  Mathematical Foundations of AI & ML<br>Unit 10: Generalization, Bias-Variance, and Model Selection
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - Mathematical Foundations of AI & ML"
---

## 01. Title: Generalization, Bias-Variance, and Model Selection
- Unit scope and role.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Evaluate and select models robustly.

## 03. Recap from Unit 9
- Optimization vs generalization distinction.

## 04. Training vs test error
- Core generalization gap concept.

## 05. IID assumption and limits
- When it breaks.

## 06. Underfitting vs overfitting
- Capacity mismatch intuition.

## 07. Bias-variance decomposition
- Error components overview.

## 08. High bias regime
- Symptoms and remedies.

## 09. High variance regime
- Symptoms and remedies.

## 10. Learning curves
- Diagnose data/model bottlenecks.

## 11. Validation set purpose
- Honest model comparison.

## 12. Cross-validation basics
- k-fold and repeated CV.

## 13. Nested CV concept
- Avoid optimistic selection bias.

## 14. Data leakage patterns
- Hidden test contamination.

## 15. Hyperparameter tuning protocol
- Search spaces and fairness.

## 16. Regularization and generalization
- Complexity control link.

## 17. Early stopping as model selection
- Implicit selection criterion.

## 18. Feature selection pitfalls
- Leakage through preprocessing.

## 19. Model complexity measures
- VC/Rademacher intuition (light).

## 20. Occam and MDL intuition
- Simplicity bias.

## 21. Distribution shift problem
- Train-test mismatch risk.

## 22. Domain adaptation preview
- Transfer considerations.

## 23. Calibration and decision quality
- Accuracy is not enough.

## 24. Uncertainty and model choice
- Risk-aware selection.

## 25. Metrics aligned to objective
- Utility-driven evaluation.

## 26. Class imbalance and metrics
- Precision-recall tradeoffs.

## 27. Statistical comparison of models
- Paired tests and intervals.

## 28. Multiple-comparison caution
- Tuning over many variants.

## 29. Ensembling effects
- Variance reduction behavior.

## 30. Stacking/blending preview
- Meta-model selection.

## 31. Reproducibility requirements
- Seeds and fixed splits.

## 32. Benchmarking hygiene
- Prevent cherry-picking.

## 33. Model cards and reporting
- Transparent selection rationale.

## 34. Real-world drift monitoring
- Post-deployment checks.

## 35. Continual reevaluation
- Trigger retraining criteria.

## 36. Materials example: sparse-data model choice
- Bias-variance in practice.

## 37. Materials example: split by chemistry family
- Generalization realism.

## 38. Materials example: uncertainty-aware selection
- Conservative deployment choices.

## 39. Common exam traps
- Confusing val/test roles.

## 40. Checklist: robust model selection
- End-to-end guardrails.

## 41. Exercise 1 scaffold
- Build learning curves.

## 42. Exercise 2 scaffold
- CV-based model comparison.

## 43. Exercise 3 scaffold
- Leakage detection and fix.

## 44. Exercise 4 scaffold
- Calibrated selection report.

## 45. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield concepts.

## 46. Summary table
- Failure modes and mitigations.

## 47. Unit summary
- Core takeaways.

## 48. Reading assignment
- Probabilistic models preview.

## 49. Transition preview
- Unit 11 roadmap.

## 50. References
- Source list.

::: {#refs}
:::
